---
title: Research Areas
subtitle: "At the crossroads of optimization, geometry, and statistics."
toc: true
title-block-banner: true
---
::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**I exploit mathematical structures to design and analyze scalable and versatile optimization algorithms, and to learn from high-dimensional data with provable guarantees.**
:::
There are a variety of problems at the foundation of Data Science, for which the best problems require ideas and techniques from multiple mathematical disciplines. Currently, I am particularly interested in using ideas from mathematical optimization, geometry, and statistics. Below are some research areas I am actively exploring.

## Riemannian Optimization 
<!-- When taught optimization, we are taught many tools and techniques for the following task: 

$$
    \min_{x \in \mathbb{R}^d} f(x),
$$

where $f : \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathbb{R}$ is some objective function. This lends itself to many different interpretations, depending on your motivations. For example -->


## Submanifolds of Wasserstein Space


## Optimization in Wasserstein Space
